notes.note: Decimus Brutus Albinus, hailed imperator after his defence of Mutina and already consul-designate for 42 BC; "IMP. COS. DESIG." abbreviates imperator, consul designatus.
Decimus Brutus had been besieged inside Mutina (modern Modena) by Antony from late 43; the siege was broken by the consular armies in April 43 BC.

notes.anchor: the thanksgivings so freshly performed at every temple of the gods
notes.note: A supplicatio, a public thanksgiving voted by the Senate at which the temples were thrown open for prayer and offering; one had just been decreed for the victory at Mutina.
Cicero's closing dateline (Latin "x iiii K. Iun.," reckoned by inclusive counting back from the Kalends of June) places the letter in late May / early June 43 BC.
